description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both made of metallic material with a matte gray finish. The blades are wide and triangular, tapering to sharp edges, and are joined at a central hub that appears reinforced. The handle is cylindrical, constructed from a dark brown wood, and is segmented with metallic bands for added grip and durability. The bottom of the handle is capped with a metallic pommel, providing balance and additional protection. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty chopping or display as a decorative or ceremonial object.
description: A double-headed battle axe with wide gray metal blades and a segmented wooden handle reinforced with metal bands and a pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical axe blades and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the pointed tip at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both blades and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and pointed top.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with segmented metal bands for grip, round cross-section, capped with a metal pommel.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, wide triangular blades with sharp edges, symmetrical, no beard or poll, functional and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible central hub reinforcement.
